cnc-electronics.wrapped_text:
CNC (Computer
Numerical Control)
Electronics
Root question: how do
CNC (Computer
Numerical Control)
electronics convert a
toolpath into safe,
accurate, diagnosable
machine motion?
cnc-electronics.wrapped_original_text:
CNC (Computer Numerical Control) Electronics
Root question: how do CNC (Computer Numerical Control) electronics convert a toolpath into safe, accurate, diagnosable machine motion?

whole-system-model.wrapped_text:
High Level System Model
Understand the machine as four
paths that meet at each axis:
command, power, feedback, and
safety.
whole-system-model.wrapped_original_text:
High Level System Model
Understand the machine as four paths that meet at each axis: command, power, feedback, and safety.

four-paths.wrapped_text:
Four Paths
Most CNC (Computer Numerical Control)
electronics problems become clearer when you
ask which path is carrying the wrong
information, energy, measurement, or
permission.
four-paths.wrapped_original_text:
Four Paths
Most CNC (Computer Numerical Control) electronics problems become clearer when you ask which path is carrying the wrong information, energy, measurement, or permission.

command-path.wrapped_text:
Command Path
G-code (machine tool programming
commands), jogs, probing moves, and
process commands become coordinated axis
motion and output states.
command-path.wrapped_original_text:
Command Path
G-code (machine tool programming commands), jogs, probing moves, and process commands become coordinated axis motion and output states.

power-path.wrapped_text:
Power Path
Mains and power supplies deliver usable energy
to drives, motors, spindle, pumps, valves, and
process tools.
power-path.wrapped_original_text:
Power Path
Mains and power supplies deliver usable energy to drives, motors, spindle, pumps, valves, and process tools.

feedback-path.wrapped_text:
Feedback Path
Encoders, scales, probes, switches, sensors,
and drive status report what actually
happened.
feedback-path.wrapped_original_text:
Feedback Path
Encoders, scales, probes, switches, sensors, and drive status report what actually happened.

safety-path.wrapped_text:
Safety Path
Safety hardware watches for conditions where motion or
process energy must stop, then permits, removes, or limits
hazardous energy without depending on the normal controller.
safety-path.wrapped_original_text:
Safety Path
Safety hardware watches for conditions where motion or process energy must stop, then permits, removes, or limits hazardous energy without depending on the normal controller.

system-boundaries.wrapped_text:
System Boundaries
Good designs keep controller decisions,
drive control, machine mechanics, and safety
authority separate.
system-boundaries.wrapped_original_text:
System Boundaries
Good designs keep controller decisions, drive control, machine mechanics, and safety authority separate.

controller-boundary.wrapped_text:
Controller Boundary
The controller owns trajectory planning,
machine state, I/O (Input/Output) logic,
homing, alarms, and command timing.
controller-boundary.wrapped_original_text:
Controller Boundary
The controller owns trajectory planning, machine state, I/O (Input/Output) logic, homing, alarms, and command timing.

drive-boundary.wrapped_text:
Drive Boundary
The drive owns power electronics, current control,
motor feedback handling, protection, and local faults.
drive-boundary.wrapped_original_text:
Drive Boundary
The drive owns power electronics, current control, motor feedback handling, protection, and local faults.

machine-boundary.wrapped_text:
Machine Boundary
The machine structure turns motor torque into
tool motion through real stiffness, backlash,
friction, and thermal growth.
machine-boundary.wrapped_original_text:
Machine Boundary
The machine structure turns motor torque into tool motion through real stiffness, backlash, friction, and thermal growth.

safety-boundary.wrapped_text:
Safety Boundary
Safety hardware should be able to remove or limit
hazardous motion without trusting the G-code
(machine tool programming commands) sender or
firmware.
safety-boundary.wrapped_original_text:
Safety Boundary
Safety hardware should be able to remove or limit hazardous motion without trusting the G-code (machine tool programming commands) sender or firmware.

controller-layer.wrapped_text:
Controller Layer
The CNC (Computer Numerical
Control) controller is the
machine-level decision maker. It
decides what motion should happen,
not how motor current is switched.
controller-layer.wrapped_original_text:
Controller Layer
The CNC (Computer Numerical Control) controller is the machine-level decision maker. It decides what motion should happen, not how motor current is switched.

controller-job.wrapped_text:
Controller Job
A controller interprets commands, plans
motion, schedules outputs, reads inputs, and
keeps the machine in a valid operating state.
controller-job.wrapped_original_text:
Controller Job
A controller interprets commands, plans motion, schedules outputs, reads inputs, and keeps the machine in a valid operating state.

program-interpretation.wrapped_text:
Program Interpretation
Tracks G-code (machine tool programming
commands) modes, units, offsets, feed modes,
spindle commands, coolant, and program flow.
program-interpretation.wrapped_original_text:
Program Interpretation
Tracks G-code (machine tool programming commands) modes, units, offsets, feed modes, spindle commands, coolant, and program flow.

trajectory-planning.wrapped_text:
Trajectory Planning
Turns geometry into time-based motion while
respecting velocity, acceleration,
cornering, and axis limits.
trajectory-planning.wrapped_original_text:
Trajectory Planning
Turns geometry into time-based motion while respecting velocity, acceleration, cornering, and axis limits.

motion-timing.wrapped_text:
Motion Timing
For step/dir systems, pulse timing is the
controller's real-time promise to the
drives.
motion-timing.wrapped_original_text:
Motion Timing
For step/dir systems, pulse timing is the controller's real-time promise to the drives.

machine-io-state.wrapped_text:
Machine I/O (Input/Output) State
Connects software state to homes, limits,
probes, relays, spindle commands,
coolant, process outputs, and alarms.
machine-io-state.wrapped_original_text:
Machine I/O (Input/Output) State
Connects software state to homes, limits, probes, relays, spindle commands, coolant, process outputs, and alarms.

alarm-state.wrapped_text:
Alarm State
Separates running, hold, homing, alarm, reset,
and safety-interlocked behavior so outputs
match machine condition.
alarm-state.wrapped_original_text:
Alarm State
Separates running, hold, homing, alarm, reset, and safety-interlocked behavior so outputs match machine condition.

controller-categories.wrapped_text:
Controller Categories
CNC (Computer Numerical Control)
controllers differ by where real-time
motion, I/O (Input/Output), safety
integration, and the operator interface
live.
controller-categories.wrapped_original_text:
Controller Categories
CNC (Computer Numerical Control) controllers differ by where real-time motion, I/O (Input/Output), safety integration, and the operator interface live.

embedded-firmware.wrapped_text:
Embedded Firmware
FluidNC and GRBL (open-source controller
firmware) style controllers are compact
step/dir controllers for small to mid-size
machines.
embedded-firmware.wrapped_original_text:
Embedded Firmware
FluidNC and GRBL (open-source controller firmware) style controllers are compact step/dir controllers for small to mid-size machines.

pc-motion-system.wrapped_text:
PC (Personal Computer) Motion System
PC (Personal Computer) systems use a
computer for interface and planning, with
real-time kernels or external timing
hardware when needed.
pc-motion-system.wrapped_original_text:
PC (Personal Computer) Motion System
PC (Personal Computer) systems use a computer for interface and planning, with real-time kernels or external timing hardware when needed.

industrial-cnc.wrapped_text:
Industrial CNC (Computer Numerical
Control)
Industrial CNCs (Computer Numerical
Control systems) integrate
high-performance motion, drives, I/O
(Input/Output), probing, diagnostics,
safety, and production workflows.
industrial-cnc.wrapped_original_text:
Industrial CNC (Computer Numerical Control)
Industrial CNCs (Computer Numerical Control systems) integrate high-performance motion, drives, I/O (Input/Output), probing, diagnostics, safety, and production workflows.

plc-motion.wrapped_text:
PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) Motion
PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) motion
systems combine automation logic, motion
function blocks, fieldbus drives,
distributed I/O (Input/Output), and safety
PLCs (Programmable Logic Controllers).
plc-motion.wrapped_original_text:
PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) Motion
PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) motion systems combine automation logic, motion function blocks, fieldbus drives, distributed I/O (Input/Output), and safety PLCs (Programmable Logic Controllers).

robot-controller.wrapped_text:
Robot Controller
Robot controllers emphasize kinematics, joint
coordination, safe zones, teach workflows, and
coordinated process tools.
robot-controller.wrapped_original_text:
Robot Controller
Robot controllers emphasize kinematics, joint coordination, safe zones, teach workflows, and coordinated process tools.

fluidnc-example.wrapped_text:
FluidNC Example
In this machine, FluidNC owns the axis
commands while the closed-loop drives own
motor feedback.
fluidnc-example.wrapped_original_text:
FluidNC Example
In this machine, FluidNC owns the axis commands while the closed-loop drives own motor feedback.

axis-allocation.wrapped_text:
Axis Allocation
Use one channel for X, one for Z, and two
for the Y gantry, leaving spare capacity on
a 6-axis controller.
axis-allocation.wrapped_original_text:
Axis Allocation
Use one channel for X, one for Z, and two for the Y gantry, leaving spare capacity on a 6-axis controller.

step-dir-interface.wrapped_text:
Step-Dir Interface
Step/dir says how far and when to move;
the drive decides how to energize the
motor.
step-dir-interface.wrapped_original_text:
Step-Dir Interface
Step/dir says how far and when to move; the drive decides how to energize the motor.

configuration-discipline.wrapped_text:
Configuration Discipline
Steps per unit, max rate, acceleration, homing
direction, limit polarity, and inversion must
match the real machine.
configuration-discipline.wrapped_original_text:
Configuration Discipline
Steps per unit, max rate, acceleration, homing direction, limit polarity, and inversion must match the real machine.

dual-y-coordination.wrapped_text:
Dual-Y Coordination
Two powered Y sides should home independently
for squaring, then move as one mechanical axis
during cutting.
dual-y-coordination.wrapped_original_text:
Dual-Y Coordination
Two powered Y sides should home independently for squaring, then move as one mechanical axis during cutting.

controller-limit.wrapped_text:
Controller Limit
FluidNC is not a safety PLC (Programmable
Logic Controller) or a high-end fieldbus CNC
(Computer Numerical Control system).
Dangerous energy needs external rated
hardware.
controller-limit.wrapped_original_text:
Controller Limit
FluidNC is not a safety PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) or a high-end fieldbus CNC (Computer Numerical Control system). Dangerous energy needs external rated hardware.

drive-motor-layer.wrapped_text:
Drive-Motor Layer
The drive-motor layer turns
command timing into torque. This
is where power electronics, motor
physics, feedback, and protection
meet.
drive-motor-layer.wrapped_original_text:
Drive-Motor Layer
The drive-motor layer turns command timing into torque. This is where power electronics, motor physics, feedback, and protection meet.

what-drive-does.wrapped_text:
What A Drive Does
A motor drive is a controlled power
amplifier between low-energy controller
commands and high-energy motor windings.
what-drive-does.wrapped_original_text:
What A Drive Does
A motor drive is a controlled power amplifier between low-energy controller commands and high-energy motor windings.

command-input.wrapped_text:
Command Input
Drives may accept step/dir pulses, analog
velocity, PWM (Pulse-Width Modulation),
serial commands, or cyclic fieldbus
setpoints.
command-input.wrapped_original_text:
Command Input
Drives may accept step/dir pulses, analog velocity, PWM (Pulse-Width Modulation), serial commands, or cyclic fieldbus setpoints.

current-control.wrapped_text:
Current Control
Motor torque comes from controlled winding
current, not from the command signal itself.
current-control.wrapped_original_text:
Current Control
Motor torque comes from controlled winding current, not from the command signal itself.

local-loops.wrapped_text:
Local Loops
Simple drivers mostly regulate current;
closed-loop stepper and servo drives also
regulate measured motion.
local-loops.wrapped_original_text:
Local Loops
Simple drivers mostly regulate current; closed-loop stepper and servo drives also regulate measured motion.

protection-and-faults.wrapped_text:
Protection And Faults
Drives detect overcurrent, voltage
faults, overheating, encoder faults,
stalls, and following error before damage
grows.
protection-and-faults.wrapped_original_text:
Protection And Faults
Drives detect overcurrent, voltage faults, overheating, encoder faults, stalls, and following error before damage grows.

drive-categories.wrapped_text:
Drive Categories
Main CNC (Computer Numerical Control) drive
families follow the motor and process
physics they control.
drive-categories.wrapped_original_text:
Drive Categories
Main CNC (Computer Numerical Control) drive families follow the motor and process physics they control.

open-loop-stepper-driver.wrapped_text:
Open-Loop Stepper Driver
Regulates phase current from commanded steps
but does not know whether the rotor
followed.
open-loop-stepper-driver.wrapped_original_text:
Open-Loop Stepper Driver
Regulates phase current from commanded steps but does not know whether the rotor followed.

closed-loop-stepper-drive.wrapped_text:
Closed-Loop Stepper Drive
Uses an encoder to detect position error,
improve behavior, and raise alarms on
lost motion.
closed-loop-stepper-drive.wrapped_original_text:
Closed-Loop Stepper Drive
Uses an encoder to detect position error, improve behavior, and raise alarms on lost motion.

servo-drive.wrapped_text:
Servo Drive
Controls a servo motor through feedback
loops for current, velocity, and position.
servo-drive.wrapped_original_text:
Servo Drive
Controls a servo motor through feedback loops for current, velocity, and position.

spindle-vfd.wrapped_text:
Spindle VFD (Variable Frequency Drive)
Controls spindle speed and torque by
modulating frequency, voltage, and sometimes
vector current.
spindle-vfd.wrapped_original_text:
Spindle VFD (Variable Frequency Drive)
Controls spindle speed and torque by modulating frequency, voltage, and sometimes vector current.

direct-and-process-drives.wrapped_text:
Direct And Process Drives
Linear motors, torque motors, plasma, laser,
waterjet, EDM (Electrical Discharge
Machining), and additive systems add
specialized drive requirements.
direct-and-process-drives.wrapped_original_text:
Direct And Process Drives
Linear motors, torque motors, plasma, laser, waterjet, EDM (Electrical Discharge Machining), and additive systems add specialized drive requirements.

stepper-driver-duties.wrapped_text:
Stepper Driver Duties
Stepper drivers hide significant
electrical engineering behind a simple
pulse input.
stepper-driver-duties.wrapped_original_text:
Stepper Driver Duties
Stepper drivers hide significant electrical engineering behind a simple pulse input.

step-and-microstep.wrapped_text:
Step And Microstep
Each step pulse advances the commanded
electrical angle; microstepping shapes phase
currents to reduce vibration.
step-and-microstep.wrapped_original_text:
Step And Microstep
Each step pulse advances the commanded electrical angle; microstepping shapes phase currents to reduce vibration.

chopper-current-control.wrapped_text:
Chopper Current Control
The driver rapidly switches the supply to
force winding current toward the requested
value.
chopper-current-control.wrapped_original_text:
Chopper Current Control
The driver rapidly switches the supply to force winding current toward the requested value.

timing-and-enable.wrapped_text:
Timing And Enable
The controller must meet step width, direction
setup, enable timing, input voltage, and input
current requirements.
timing-and-enable.wrapped_original_text:
Timing And Enable
The controller must meet step width, direction setup, enable timing, input voltage, and input current requirements.

alarm-reporting.wrapped_text:
Alarm Reporting
Fault outputs let the controller stop the
whole machine when one axis loses the ability
to follow.
alarm-reporting.wrapped_original_text:
Alarm Reporting
Fault outputs let the controller stop the whole machine when one axis loses the ability to follow.

open-vs-closed-loop.wrapped_text:
Open Vs Closed Loop
The key difference is whether actual motion is
measured and used, not whether the motor is
physically a stepper.
open-vs-closed-loop.wrapped_original_text:
Open Vs Closed Loop
The key difference is whether actual motion is measured and used, not whether the motor is physically a stepper.

open-loop-steppers.wrapped_text:
Open-Loop Steppers
Open-loop steppers are simple and predictable
when torque margin is high and disturbances
are controlled.
open-loop-steppers.wrapped_original_text:
Open-Loop Steppers
Open-loop steppers are simple and predictable when torque margin is high and disturbances are controlled.

missed-step-failure.wrapped_text:
Missed Step Failure
An open-loop missed step can remain invisible
until the part, fixture, or machine position
is wrong.
missed-step-failure.wrapped_original_text:
Missed Step Failure
An open-loop missed step can remain invisible until the part, fixture, or machine position is wrong.

closed-loop-steppers.wrapped_text:
Closed-Loop Steppers
Closed-loop steppers add encoder-based
correction, stall detection, and
following-error alarms while keeping
step/dir control.
closed-loop-steppers.wrapped_original_text:
Closed-Loop Steppers
Closed-loop steppers add encoder-based correction, stall detection, and following-error alarms while keeping step/dir control.

following-error.wrapped_text:
Following Error
Following error is the gap between commanded
and measured position. It is both a tuning
signal and a fault signal.
following-error.wrapped_original_text:
Following Error
Following error is the gap between commanded and measured position. It is both a tuning signal and a fault signal.

motor-physics.wrapped_text:
Motor Physics
Motor sizing is about torque across speed,
thermal limits, inertia, and the
mechanical load, not holding torque alone.
motor-physics.wrapped_original_text:
Motor Physics
Motor sizing is about torque across speed, thermal limits, inertia, and the mechanical load, not holding torque alone.

torque-current-link.wrapped_text:
Torque Current Link
Stepper and servo torque depends on controlled
winding current and magnetic alignment.
torque-current-link.wrapped_original_text:
Torque Current Link
Stepper and servo torque depends on controlled winding current and magnetic alignment.

inductance-and-back-emf.wrapped_text:
Inductance And Back EMF (Electromotive Force)
Inductance slows current rise, and back EMF
(Electromotive Force) at speed reduces voltage
headroom for current control.
inductance-and-back-emf.wrapped_original_text:
Inductance And Back EMF (Electromotive Force)
Inductance slows current rise, and back EMF (Electromotive Force) at speed reduces voltage headroom for current control.

thermal-budget.wrapped_text:
Thermal Budget
Current setting, duty cycle, cooling,
enclosure temperature, and drive losses
determine safe continuous operation.
thermal-budget.wrapped_original_text:
Thermal Budget
Current setting, duty cycle, cooling, enclosure temperature, and drive losses determine safe continuous operation.

example-drives.wrapped_text:
Example Drives
The example machine uses four closed-loop
encoder drives, one per NEMA (National
Electrical Manufacturers Association) 23
motor.
example-drives.wrapped_original_text:
Example Drives
The example machine uses four closed-loop encoder drives, one per NEMA (National Electrical Manufacturers Association) 23 motor.

per-axis-tuning.wrapped_text:
Per-Axis Tuning
X, Z, and both Y sides should be
current-limited and tuned for their
actual mechanics.
per-axis-tuning.wrapped_original_text:
Per-Axis Tuning
X, Z, and both Y sides should be current-limited and tuned for their actual mechanics.

dual-y-fault-stop.wrapped_text:
Dual-Y Fault Stop
If either Y drive faults while the other keeps
driving, the gantry can rack and damage
itself.
dual-y-fault-stop.wrapped_original_text:
Dual-Y Fault Stop
If either Y drive faults while the other keeps driving, the gantry can rack and damage itself.

feedback-and-accuracy.wrapped_text:
Feedback And Accuracy
Feedback is only useful when you
know what it measures: motor
shaft, axis position, tool
position, process state, or part
quality.
feedback-and-accuracy.wrapped_original_text:
Feedback And Accuracy
Feedback is only useful when you know what it measures: motor shaft, axis position, tool position, process state, or part quality.

feedback-levels.wrapped_text:
Feedback Levels
High-end systems push measurement closer to
the real machining result.
feedback-levels.wrapped_original_text:
Feedback Levels
High-end systems push measurement closer to the real machining result.

motor-encoder.wrapped_text:
Motor Encoder
Measures motor shaft motion and helps the
drive detect stalls or regulate servo loops.
motor-encoder.wrapped_original_text:
Motor Encoder
Measures motor shaft motion and helps the drive detect stalls or regulate servo loops.

axis-scale.wrapped_text:
Axis Scale
Measures the actual axis after some
screw, belt, rack, or gearbox error has
already happened.
axis-scale.wrapped_original_text:
Axis Scale
Measures the actual axis after some screw, belt, rack, or gearbox error has already happened.

spindle-feedback.wrapped_text:
Spindle Feedback
Enables rigid tapping, threading, spindle
orientation, speed verification, and
synchronized process control.
spindle-feedback.wrapped_original_text:
Spindle Feedback
Enables rigid tapping, threading, spindle orientation, speed verification, and synchronized process control.

probe-and-process-feedback.wrapped_text:
Probe And Process Feedback
Probes, tool setters, arc voltage,
laser height, pressure, load,
vibration, and temperature connect
control to the real process.
probe-and-process-feedback.wrapped_original_text:
Probe And Process Feedback
Probes, tool setters, arc voltage, laser height, pressure, load, vibration, and temperature connect control to the real process.

loop-placement.wrapped_text:
Loop Placement
Where the loop closes determines what errors
electronics can correct.
loop-placement.wrapped_original_text:
Loop Placement
Where the loop closes determines what errors electronics can correct.

drive-loop.wrapped_text:
Drive Loop
Closed-loop stepper drives usually close the
loop at the motor, so they do not see screw
error or gantry skew.
drive-loop.wrapped_original_text:
Drive Loop
Closed-loop stepper drives usually close the loop at the motor, so they do not see screw error or gantry skew.

controller-or-metrology-loop.wrapped_text:
Controller Or Metrology Loop
Industrial controllers may close
axis-scale loops or use probing and
compensation to correct machine and part
error.
controller-or-metrology-loop.wrapped_original_text:
Controller Or Metrology Loop
Industrial controllers may close axis-scale loops or use probing and compensation to correct machine and part error.

accuracy-stack.wrapped_text:
Accuracy Stack
Part accuracy is the stack-up of
electronics, mechanics, heat, tooling, and
process force.
accuracy-stack.wrapped_original_text:
Accuracy Stack
Part accuracy is the stack-up of electronics, mechanics, heat, tooling, and process force.

resolution-is-not-accuracy.wrapped_text:
Resolution Is Not Accuracy
Steps per unit and encoder counts set command
granularity, but they do not prove the tool is
where you think it is.
resolution-is-not-accuracy.wrapped_original_text:
Resolution Is Not Accuracy
Steps per unit and encoder counts set command granularity, but they do not prove the tool is where you think it is.

mechanical-error.wrapped_text:
Mechanical Error
Belts, screws, racks, couplers, bearings,
and frames add backlash, pitch error,
compliance, and lost motion.
mechanical-error.wrapped_original_text:
Mechanical Error
Belts, screws, racks, couplers, bearings, and frames add backlash, pitch error, compliance, and lost motion.

thermal-and-process-error.wrapped_text:
Thermal And Process Error
Spindle growth, screw heating, frame
expansion, cutting forces, and process forces
move the real tool path.
thermal-and-process-error.wrapped_original_text:
Thermal And Process Error
Spindle growth, screw heating, frame expansion, cutting forces, and process forces move the real tool path.

gantry-accuracy.wrapped_text:
Gantry Accuracy
The example dual-Y gantry makes accuracy a
geometry problem as much as an
axis-control problem.
gantry-accuracy.wrapped_original_text:
Gantry Accuracy
The example dual-Y gantry makes accuracy a geometry problem as much as an axis-control problem.

independent-y-homes.wrapped_text:
Independent Y Homes
Each Y side should have its own home reference
so the gantry can square before cutting.
independent-y-homes.wrapped_original_text:
Independent Y Homes
Each Y side should have its own home reference so the gantry can square before cutting.

hidden-gantry-error.wrapped_text:
Hidden Gantry Error
Motor encoders can report perfect tracking
while belts, racks, or the gantry
structure still deflect.
hidden-gantry-error.wrapped_original_text:
Hidden Gantry Error
Motor encoders can report perfect tracking while belts, racks, or the gantry structure still deflect.

cabinet-and-wiring.wrapped_text:
Cabinet And Wiring
The cabinet determines whether the
electronics behave like a
controlled instrument or a noise
antenna.
cabinet-and-wiring.wrapped_original_text:
Cabinet And Wiring
The cabinet determines whether the electronics behave like a controlled instrument or a noise antenna.

power-supplies.wrapped_text:
Power Supplies
Separate supply roles make the system easier
to size, protect, and diagnose.
power-supplies.wrapped_original_text:
Power Supplies
Separate supply roles make the system easier to size, protect, and diagnose.

motor-bus-supply.wrapped_text:
Motor Bus Supply
The motor supply must support speed,
acceleration, current demand, heating,
and drive voltage limits.
motor-bus-supply.wrapped_original_text:
Motor Bus Supply
The motor supply must support speed, acceleration, current demand, heating, and drive voltage limits.

control-and-logic-power.wrapped_text:
Control And Logic Power
Industrial controls commonly use 24 V for
sensors and relays, while controllers and some
drive inputs may need compatible 5 V logic.
control-and-logic-power.wrapped_original_text:
Control And Logic Power
Industrial controls commonly use 24 V for sensors and relays, while controllers and some drive inputs may need compatible 5 V logic.

braking-energy.wrapped_text:
Braking Energy
Decelerating motors and spindles can return
energy to the bus, requiring braking
resistors, regeneration, or gentler decel
limits.
braking-energy.wrapped_original_text:
Braking Energy
Decelerating motors and spindles can return energy to the bus, requiring braking resistors, regeneration, or gentler decel limits.

grounding-and-bonding.wrapped_text:
Grounding And Bonding
Grounding is for fault safety and reference
control. Shielding is for noise control.
They touch, but they are not the same idea.
grounding-and-bonding.wrapped_original_text:
Grounding And Bonding
Grounding is for fault safety and reference control. Shielding is for noise control. They touch, but they are not the same idea.

protective-earth.wrapped_text:
Protective Earth
Bond the cabinet, machine frame, motor cases,
cable shields, and exposed conductive parts to
protective earth.
protective-earth.wrapped_original_text:
Protective Earth
Bond the cabinet, machine frame, motor cases, cable shields, and exposed conductive parts to protective earth.

signal-common.wrapped_text:
Signal Common
Logic common is a circuit reference and should
be connected deliberately, not accidentally
through random cables.
signal-common.wrapped_original_text:
Signal Common
Logic common is a circuit reference and should be connected deliberately, not accidentally through random cables.

shield-bonding-trap.wrapped_text:
Shield Bonding Trap
Random shield and common connections can
create noise currents through sensitive signal
references.
shield-bonding-trap.wrapped_original_text:
Shield Bonding Trap
Random shield and common connections can create noise currents through sensitive signal references.

shielding-and-routing.wrapped_text:
Shielding And Routing
Physical separation and shield termination
matter before filters and software debounce.
shielding-and-routing.wrapped_original_text:
Shielding And Routing
Physical separation and shield termination matter before filters and software debounce.

power-cable-routing.wrapped_text:
Power Cable Routing
Motor phase and VFD (Variable Frequency Drive)
cables carry fast switching current and should
be separated from encoder, probe, and sensor
wiring.
power-cable-routing.wrapped_original_text:
Power Cable Routing
Motor phase and VFD (Variable Frequency Drive) cables carry fast switching current and should be separated from encoder, probe, and sensor wiring.

feedback-cable-routing.wrapped_text:
Feedback Cable Routing
Encoder and sensor cables need twisted
pairs, shield continuity, connector quality,
and distance from power wiring.
feedback-cable-routing.wrapped_original_text:
Feedback Cable Routing
Encoder and sensor cables need twisted pairs, shield continuity, connector quality, and distance from power wiring.

cable-segregation.wrapped_text:
Cable Segregation
Plan separate routes for high-power switching,
low-level feedback, safety, and communications
wiring.
cable-segregation.wrapped_original_text:
Cable Segregation
Plan separate routes for high-power switching, low-level feedback, safety, and communications wiring.

noise-immunity.wrapped_text:
Noise Immunity
Noise immunity comes from reducing
emissions, reducing coupling, and making
receivers more tolerant.
noise-immunity.wrapped_original_text:
Noise Immunity
Noise immunity comes from reducing emissions, reducing coupling, and making receivers more tolerant.

differential-signaling.wrapped_text:
Differential Signaling
Differential encoder, RS-485 (differential
serial communication standard), and fieldbus
signals reject common-mode noise better than
single-ended cabinet wiring.
differential-signaling.wrapped_original_text:
Differential Signaling
Differential encoder, RS-485 (differential serial communication standard), and fieldbus signals reject common-mode noise better than single-ended cabinet wiring.

isolation-and-filtering.wrapped_text:
Isolation And Filtering
Opto isolation, debounce, filters, ferrites,
snubbers, and surge suppressors help only when
matched to the actual noise path.
isolation-and-filtering.wrapped_original_text:
Isolation And Filtering
Opto isolation, debounce, filters, ferrites, snubbers, and surge suppressors help only when matched to the actual noise path.

example-cabinet-priorities.wrapped_text:
Example Cabinet Priorities
For the FluidNC closed-loop stepper machine,
cabinet basics matter more than exotic
electronics.
example-cabinet-priorities.wrapped_original_text:
Example Cabinet Priorities

voltage, polarity, timing, and input current
requirements.

Wire For Diagnosis

states visible.

safety-and-industrial-practice.wrapped_text:
Safety And Industrial Practice
Industrial best practice scales
CNC (Computer Numerical Control)
electronics with rated safety,
deterministic timing, rich
diagnostics, servo performance,
and metrology feedback.
safety-and-industrial-practice.wrapped_original_text:
Safety And Industrial Practice
Industrial best practice scales CNC (Computer Numerical Control) electronics with rated safety, deterministic timing, rich diagnostics, servo performance, and metrology feedback.

limits-homing-alarms.wrapped_text:
Limits Homing Alarms
Limits, homing, and alarms define the
machine's legal operating envelope.
limits-homing-alarms.wrapped_original_text:
Limits Homing Alarms
Limits, homing, and alarms define the machine's legal operating envelope.

home-vs-limit.wrapped_text:
Home Vs Limit
Home switches establish coordinates; limit
switches protect boundaries. Mixing those jobs
causes confusion.
home-vs-limit.wrapped_original_text:
Home Vs Limit
Home switches establish coordinates; limit switches protect boundaries. Mixing those jobs causes confusion.

soft-and-hard-limits.wrapped_text:
Soft And Hard Limits
Soft limits reject impossible moves after
homing; hard limits react to physical boundary
inputs.
soft-and-hard-limits.wrapped_original_text:
Soft And Hard Limits
Soft limits reject impossible moves after homing; hard limits react to physical boundary inputs.

alarm-chain.wrapped_text:
Alarm Chain
Drive, process, limit, and safety faults
should produce clear machine states and
operator messages.
alarm-chain.wrapped_original_text:
Alarm Chain

emergency-stop.wrapped_text:
Emergency Stop
Emergency stop is a safety function design
problem, not just a spare controller
input.
emergency-stop.wrapped_original_text:
Emergency Stop
Emergency stop is a safety function design problem, not just a spare controller input.

Feed Hold Is Not E-Stop (Emergency Stop)
Feed hold, pause, reset, or sender
disconnect are operational controls, not
rated emergency stop functions.
feed-hold-is-not-e-stop.wrapped_original_text:
Feed Hold Is Not E-Stop (Emergency Stop)

stop-and-reset-behavior.wrapped_text:
Stop And Reset Behavior
Stop category 0 removes torque immediately;
stop category 1 decelerates first; reset
must not automatically restart hazards.
stop-and-reset-behavior.wrapped_original_text:
Stop And Reset Behavior
Stop category 0 removes torque immediately; stop category 1 decelerates first; reset must not automatically restart hazards.

safety-rated-motion.wrapped_text:
Safety-Rated Motion

hazardous motion with defined safety
functions.
safety-rated-motion.wrapped_original_text:
Safety-Rated Motion
Modern drives and safety controllers can limit hazardous motion with defined safety functions.

sto-and-ss1.wrapped_text:
STO (Safe Torque Off) And SS1 (Safe Stop 1)
Safe Torque Off prevents torque-producing
output, while Safe Stop 1 stops first and then
removes torque.
sto-and-ss1.wrapped_original_text:
STO (Safe Torque Off) And SS1 (Safe Stop 1)

sls-and-sos.wrapped_text:
SLS (Safely Limited Speed) And SOS (Safe
Operating Stop)
Safely Limited Speed permits guarded slow
motion; Safe Operating Stop monitors
standstill while holding position.

SLS (Safely Limited Speed) And SOS (Safe Operating Stop)

discipline, stop-time proof, reset testing,
and maintenance evidence.

servo-practice.wrapped_text:
Servo Practice

machines use servos when dynamic stiffness,
speed range, duty cycle, and fault
observability matter.

Servo Practice

cascaded-loops.wrapped_text:
Cascaded Loops
Current, velocity, and position loops are
tuned as a nested system, not as independent
knobs.

Cascaded Loops
Current, velocity, and position loops are tuned as a nested system, not as independent knobs.

Feedforward And Inertia
Feedforward, friction compensation, gravity
compensation, and inertia matching reduce
error before feedback reacts.

fieldbus-motion.wrapped_text:
Fieldbus Motion
Fieldbus motion replaces loose pulse
wiring with cyclic command, feedback,
state, and diagnostics data.

Fieldbus Motion

motion-networks.wrapped_text:
Motion Networks
EtherCAT (Ethernet for Control
Automation Technology), Profinet IRT
(Isochronous Real Time), Sercos
(Serial Real-time Communication
System), and MECHATROLINK (industrial
motion network) are used for
synchronized industrial motion.
motion-networks.wrapped_original_text:
Motion Networks
EtherCAT (Ethernet for Control Automation Technology), Profinet IRT (Isochronous Real Time), Sercos (Serial Real-time Communication System), and MECHATROLINK (industrial motion network) are used for synchronized industrial motion.

402 Drive Profile

402 standardizes drive state, control word,
status word, operation modes, and cyclic
process data.

Deterministic Timing
Cycle time, jitter, and synchronized
feedback determine whether axes receive
time-aligned commands and measurements.

diagnostics-and-metrology.wrapped_text:
Diagnostics And Metrology
Diagnostics explain what failed; metrology
explains what the machine or part actually
did.
diagnostics-and-metrology.wrapped_original_text:
Diagnostics And Metrology
Diagnostics explain what failed; metrology explains what the machine or part actually did.

drive-trace-and-alarms.wrapped_text:
Drive Trace And Alarms
Industrial drives can record current, speed,
position, following error, bus voltage, and
fault bits around an event.
drive-trace-and-alarms.wrapped_original_text:

Commissioning Record
Keep motor parameters, tuning, I/O

settings, and safety validation records.

Commissioning Record

metrology-feedback.wrapped_text:

Linear scales, probing, tool setters, and
thermal compensation close the loop
around machine truth and part truth.

feedback.

fire or arc hazards.
plasma-laser-waterjet.wrapped_original_text:
Plasma Laser Waterjet
Plasma, laser, and waterjet machines emphasize process enable safety, height control, EMI (Electromagnetic Interference), pressure, and fire or arc hazards.

additive-robotics.wrapped_text:
Additive Robotics
Additive and robotic systems emphasize

collision zones, sensor fusion, and human
interaction safety.

Additive Robotics

upgrade-path.wrapped_text:
Upgrade Path
The FluidNC closed-loop stepper machine is a
good learning platform; industrial machines
move more functions into rated hardware and
deterministic networks.
upgrade-path.wrapped_original_text:
Upgrade Path

tests.

Industrial upgrades usually combine servo
sizing, fieldbus diagnostics, drive
safety functions, and formal validation.
servo-fieldbus-safety.wrapped_original_text:
Servo Fieldbus Safety
Industrial upgrades usually combine servo sizing, fieldbus diagnostics, drive safety functions, and formal validation.

hazard-inputs.wrapped_text:
Hazard Inputs
Examples include emergency-stop buttons,
guard-door switches, hard limits, drive safety
feedback, spindle faults, air-pressure faults,
and process interlocks.
hazard-inputs.wrapped_original_text:
Hazard Inputs
Examples include emergency-stop buttons, guard-door switches, hard limits, drive safety feedback, spindle faults, air-pressure faults, and process interlocks.

safety-decision.wrapped_text:
Safety Decision
A safety relay, safety PLC (Programmable
Logic Controller), or rated drive
function checks the input circuit, reset
condition, and feedback contacts before
allowing hazardous outputs.
safety-decision.wrapped_original_text:
Safety Decision
A safety relay, safety PLC (Programmable Logic Controller), or rated drive function checks the input circuit, reset condition, and feedback contacts before allowing hazardous outputs.

energy-control.wrapped_text:
Energy Control
Typical outputs remove or limit torque through
STO (Safe Torque Off), drop motor or spindle
contactors, disable process energy, close
valves, or command a controlled safe stop.
energy-control.wrapped_original_text:
Energy Control
Typical outputs remove or limit torque through STO (Safe Torque Off), drop motor or spindle contactors, disable process energy, close valves, or command a controlled safe stop.

restart-control.wrapped_text:
Restart Control
After a safety trip, reset should prove
the hazard is cleared and require a
deliberate restart; releasing the E-stop
(Emergency Stop) should not automatically
resume motion.
restart-control.wrapped_original_text:
Restart Control
After a safety trip, reset should prove the hazard is cleared and require a deliberate restart; releasing the E-stop (Emergency Stop) should not automatically resume motion.

controller-boundary-pitfall.wrapped_text:
Controller Boundary Pitfall
A common bad design is making the CNC (Computer Numerical
Control) controller responsible for things it can only request, not
guarantee. In a DIY (Do-It-Yourself) machine, this happens when an E-
stop (Emergency Stop), spindle permissive, or hard limit is treated as
just another FluidNC input. The firmware may stop sending steps, but
if the controller crashes, USB (Universal Serial Bus) drops, an output
transistor fails, or a relay is wired normally-open in the wrong place,
the drive enable or spindle run signal can remain in an unsafe state.

Prevent this by keeping the controller's job focused on machine state
and coordinated motion. Let it read limits, homes, drive alarms,
probes, and process faults for diagnosis and orderly stops, but do not
make it the only authority over hazardous energy. Use fail-safe input
wiring, define clear alarm and reset states, bring fault signals back into
the controller, and use external safety hardware where stopping must
still work when the controller is wrong.
controller-boundary-pitfall.wrapped_original_text:
Controller Boundary Pitfall
A common bad design is making the CNC (Computer Numerical Control) controller responsible for things it can only request, not guarantee. In a DIY (Do-It-Yourself) machine, this happens when an E-stop (Emergency Stop), spindle permissive, or hard limit is treated as just another FluidNC input. The firmware may stop sending steps, but if the controller crashes, USB (Universal Serial Bus) drops, an output transistor fails, or a relay is wired normally-open in the wrong place, the drive enable or spindle run signal can remain in an unsafe state.

Prevent this by keeping the controller's job focused on machine state and coordinated motion. Let it read limits, homes, drive alarms, probes, and process faults for diagnosis and orderly stops, but do not make it the only authority over hazardous energy. Use fail-safe input wiring, define clear alarm and reset states, bring fault signals back into the controller, and use external safety hardware where stopping must still work when the controller is wrong.

drive-boundary-pitfall.wrapped_text:
Drive Boundary Pitfall
Bad drive boundaries show up when the controller treats a drive as a
silent pulse amplifier. A closed-loop stepper drive may detect following
error, encoder loss, overcurrent, or overheating, but if its alarm output is
not wired into the controller or safety chain, the rest of the machine can
continue as if the axis still exists. On a dual-Y router, one Y drive
faulting while the other side keeps driving can rack the gantry, twist the
frame, or ruin a fixture before the operator understands the fault.

Prevent this by wiring every drive's alarm, ready, and enable behavior
deliberately. Confirm voltage levels, polarity, pullups, step timing, enable
timing, and what the drive does after a fault. For coordinated axes, test
one-axis fault cases during commissioning and make the intended stop
behavior explicit. Also separate ordinary drive enable from safety-rated
functions: a normal enable input may be useful for control, but STO
(Safe Torque Off) or a rated safety device is the boundary for
hazardous torque removal.
drive-boundary-pitfall.wrapped_original_text:
Drive Boundary Pitfall
Bad drive boundaries show up when the controller treats a drive as a silent pulse amplifier. A closed-loop stepper drive may detect following error, encoder loss, overcurrent, or overheating, but if its alarm output is not wired into the controller or safety chain, the rest of the machine can continue as if the axis still exists. On a dual-Y router, one Y drive faulting while the other side keeps driving can rack the gantry, twist the frame, or ruin a fixture before the operator understands the fault.

Prevent this by wiring every drive's alarm, ready, and enable behavior deliberately. Confirm voltage levels, polarity, pullups, step timing, enable timing, and what the drive does after a fault. For coordinated axes, test one-axis fault cases during commissioning and make the intended stop behavior explicit. Also separate ordinary drive enable from safety-rated functions: a normal enable input may be useful for control, but STO (Safe Torque Off) or a rated safety device is the boundary for hazardous torque removal.

machine-boundary-pitfall.wrapped_text:
Machine Boundary Pitfall
A common boundary mistake is expecting electronics to fix mechanical
truth. Increasing steps per unit, adding motor encoders, or tightening
following-error limits does not remove a loose coupler, belt stretch,
rack backlash, gantry skew, spindle growth, or frame deflection. A
motor encoder can report perfect tracking while the cutter is still off
because the error happened after the motor shaft.

Prevent this by treating mechanical errors at the machine boundary
first. Fix lost motion, alignment, stiffness, belt tension, screw support,
bearing play, and gantry squaring before relying on software
compensation. Use independent homes for dual drives when squaring
matters, and reserve probing, axis scales, backlash compensation,
and thermal compensation for errors that are repeatable and
measured. If the machine changes under cutting load or heat, the
controller needs real feedback or conservative process limits, not just
more resolution.
machine-boundary-pitfall.wrapped_original_text:
Machine Boundary Pitfall
A common boundary mistake is expecting electronics to fix mechanical truth. Increasing steps per unit, adding motor encoders, or tightening following-error limits does not remove a loose coupler, belt stretch, rack backlash, gantry skew, spindle growth, or frame deflection. A motor encoder can report perfect tracking while the cutter is still off because the error happened after the motor shaft.

Prevent this by treating mechanical errors at the machine boundary first. Fix lost motion, alignment, stiffness, belt tension, screw support, bearing play, and gantry squaring before relying on software compensation. Use independent homes for dual drives when squaring matters, and reserve probing, axis scales, backlash compensation, and thermal compensation for errors that are repeatable and measured. If the machine changes under cutting load or heat, the controller needs real feedback or conservative process limits, not just more resolution.

safety-boundary-pitfall.wrapped_text:
Safety Boundary Pitfall
The worst boundary mistake is confusing an operational stop with a safety
function. Feed hold, reset, sender disconnect, controller reboot, and a normal
input pin can be useful controls, but they are not enough for emergency stop,
guard doors, process energy, or hazardous stored energy. In real machines
this shows up as guard switches hidden in ordinary PLC (Programmable Logic
Controller) logic, a VFD (Variable Frequency Drive) fault ignored by the CNC
(Computer Numerical Control), a brake released by a convenience output, or
an E-stop (Emergency Stop) that stops axis commands while the spindle,
plasma arc, coolant pump, or pneumatic actuator can still energize.

Prevent this with a safety path that can act independently of normal control.
Use normally-closed safety circuits, safety relays or safety PLCs
(Programmable Logic Controllers) where risk requires them, drive STO (Safe
Torque Off) or appropriate contactors, feedback contacts for contactor
monitoring, and a deliberate reset that cannot automatically restart motion. Test
the actual result: press E-stop (Emergency Stop), open guards, fault a drive,
remove air pressure, and verify which energy sources are removed, which
axes coast or brake, what the controller reports, and what the operator must do
before restart.
safety-boundary-pitfall.wrapped_original_text:
Safety Boundary Pitfall
The worst boundary mistake is confusing an operational stop with a safety function. Feed hold, reset, sender disconnect, controller reboot, and a normal input pin can be useful controls, but they are not enough for emergency stop, guard doors, process energy, or hazardous stored energy. In real machines this shows up as guard switches hidden in ordinary PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) logic, a VFD (Variable Frequency Drive) fault ignored by the CNC (Computer Numerical Control), a brake released by a convenience output, or an E-stop (Emergency Stop) that stops axis commands while the spindle, plasma arc, coolant pump, or pneumatic actuator can still energize.

Prevent this with a safety path that can act independently of normal control. Use normally-closed safety circuits, safety relays or safety PLCs (Programmable Logic Controllers) where risk requires them, drive STO (Safe Torque Off) or appropriate contactors, feedback contacts for contactor monitoring, and a deliberate reset that cannot automatically restart motion. Test the actual result: press E-stop (Emergency Stop), open guards, fault a drive, remove air pressure, and verify which energy sources are removed, which axes coast or brake, what the controller reports, and what the operator must do before restart.
